title: Capstone Case Study 1 - Phase 4 System Integration Testing (SIT)

sections.content: Configuration has been completed and transported into the QA environment. The objective of SIT is to validate that all integrated business processes work correctly across SAP S/4HANA, SAP TM, SAP EWM and Finance.

sections.title: Defect Log
rows: Freight Unit not generated for export deliveries

sections.background: The project manager asks you to classify each defect before assigning it to a support team.
questions: Which defect is caused by master data?
Which defect is caused by configuration?
Which defect is an integration issue?

sections.title: Go-Live Decision
sections.question: Can the project proceed to UAT with DEF-101 still unresolved?

options.text: No. P1 defects must be resolved before UAT.
Ignore the issue because only export deliveries are affected.

sections.explanation: A P1 defect affecting Freight Unit generation blocks a core business process and must be resolved before User Acceptance Testing.

notes: Business sign-off postponed until P1 closure.

sections.title: Senior Consultant Advice
sections.content: Never classify every issue as a configuration defect. First determine whether the root cause lies in master data, integration, business process, authorization or configuration. Accurate classification speeds up resolution.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: During an SAP TM rollout, a P1 defect prevented Freight Unit generation for export deliveries. Investigation revealed that export shipping conditions were missing from master data rather than the planning configuration. Correcting the master data resolved the issue without any code changes.

sections.title: Accenture SIT Question
sections.content: If a P1 defect is found during SIT, should UAT continue? Explain your reasoning. A consultant should discuss business impact, defect severity, exit criteria and project risk before making a recommendation.

sections.title: Phase 4 Complete
sections.content: System Integration Testing has been completed. Critical defects have been analyzed, classified and resolved. The project is now ready to begin User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with business users.
